data-fontsize-level="1"><p>Copper prices remain firm despite geopolitical tensions, with LME three-month copper recently hitting $13,637 per tonne. Citi forecasts prices could reach $15,000 by year-end, supported by energy transition, AI, and military demand, along with supply constraints. The Strait of Hormuz closure is impacting scrap recycling. Even with suppressed global demand, copper is expected to stay above $12,000 per ton through Q2. Structural demand, particularly for EVs and renewables, is mitigating risks from high oil prices and potential economic downturns. Military consumption and government inventory build-ups are additional price drivers.</p></div><div class="aiSummaryFooter-a77f5177"><span class="aiSummaryBadge-fa40812d">AI-generated summary</span></div></div></div></div></div><div class="blurMaskContent-d2e5a3d0" style="max-height:none"><div><div><div class="poster-ec765e2b"><img src="https://resource.tradingkey.com/uploads/20251024/Copper-8075833715c04b39a9e901f39c7d3a36.jpg" alt=""/></div><div class="content-b63adfce" data-fontsize-level="1"><div class="contentText-f9a0dc29"><div id="thirdPartyWrapContainer"><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">TradingKey - Despite the prolonged U.S.-Iran war and the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz, copper price bottoms remain firm. This Monday, LME (London Metal Exchange) three-month copper touched $13,637 per tonne, reaching its highest level since January 29. On that day, LME three-month copper recorded a closing high of $13,952 per tonne, having briefly surged above $14,500 per tonne during the session.</span></p><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">Citi's latest report noted that the copper market continues to demonstrate resilience under the pressure of war and ongoing blockades. Citi maintained its near-term price target at $13,000 per tonne and predicted that once the strait reopens and market sentiment improves, copper prices will rise to $15,000 per tonne by year-end.</span></p><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">Citi believes that the reason copper has not fallen significantly despite the war is primarily due to support from three factors: the energy transition, growth in AI and military demand, and supply-side constraints.</span></p></div></div><h2 id="cfmiuzjljs" class="contentTitle-b18c0982"><span></span>Copper Price Forecast: $12,000 Floor in Q2, Potential Surge to $15,000 by Year-End</h2><div class="contentText-f9a0dc29"><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">In the report, Citi forecasts that under a base-case scenario, the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will persist until the end of May. Amid suppressed global growth demand, even a more significant sell-off in risk assets would likely see dip-buying in the copper market support prices above $12,000 per ton through the second quarter.</span></p><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">In a bull-case scenario, Citi predicts that if the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz drives improved growth expectations and the release of restocking demand, or if the crisis provides a structural boost to energy transition demand, average copper prices could reach $15,000 per ton by year-end. In a bear-case scenario, Citi expects copper prices to fall to $10,000 per ton, but assigns this outcome a weighting of only 20%.</span></p></div><h2 id="kgdqupqezw" class="contentTitle-b18c0982"><span></span>Why High Oil Prices are Failing to Hinder the Rise in Copper Prices?</h2><div class="contentText-f9a0dc29"><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">Generally, there is a negative correlation between oil prices and copper prices, as rising oil prices significantly increase manufacturing costs and stifle global economic growth. Copper is regarded as a bellwether for global industrial activity; when economic growth is hit, demand for industrial copper also declines, exerting downward pressure on copper prices. However, in this round of conflict, copper prices have continued to rise overall, gaining approximately 10% since late 2025.</span></p><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">Analysis indicates that the primary reason is that the expansion of structural demand for copper has substantially mitigated the downward risk to total copper demand. Historical data shows that during major economic downturns, such as the second oil shock in the 1980s and the 2008 global financial crisis, copper demand fell by an average of 3% to 5% annually. According to Citi's estimates, assuming structural demand remains constant, if cyclical demand drops by 5%, global refined copper consumption would only decrease by about 1.7%; if cyclical demand falls by just 3%, total global demand would remain largely flat.</span></p><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">Citi even pointed out that, in some cases, high oil prices do not lead to a contraction in copper demand; instead, they may drive copper demand higher by accelerating the energy transition. For instance, under such geopolitical pressure, fuel-importing nations may promote the adoption of renewable energy, naturally increasing copper demand for electric vehicles, energy storage, and power grid infrastructure.</span></p></div><h2 id="vfhircibwl" class="contentTitle-b18c0982"><span></span>Military demand provides additional upside potential for copper prices.</h2><div class="contentText-f9a0dc29"><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">Citi noted another factor in its report: the support for copper prices from military demand. According to Citi's estimates, global military-related copper consumption is approximately 2.5 million tons per year, accounting for about 9% of total global consumption. Furthermore, a global consensus has emerged that international defense budgets will maintain moderate growth, which will support military copper demand remaining at elevated levels.</span></p><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">The equipment intensity of modern warfare continues to increase, with the extensive use of consumable hardware such as drones and missiles accelerating copper consumption. Historical data suggests that following the outbreak of the Russia-Ukraine war, the growth rate of military copper consumption may outpace the growth rate of actual military expenditures.</span></p></div><h2 id="gqkmatqkik" class="contentTitle-b18c0982"><span></span>Surging Energy Costs Pressure Scrap Copper Recycling</h2><div class="contentText-f9a0dc29"><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">While copper demand undergoes structural reshaping, its supply side is also being impacted by strait closures. Currently, copper is sourced from two channels: copper scrap recycling and primary copper ore production. Since the beginning of the year, copper mine production expectations have remained largely flat, making scrap recovery and recycling the primary factor influencing copper output.</span></p><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">However, the collection, processing, and remelting of copper scrap are highly dependent on energy and transportation, making a closure of the Strait of Hormuz a critical factor affecting copper production. Such a closure would not only drive up energy prices but also push up transport insurance premiums, fuel surcharges, and other transit fees, thereby dampening suppliers' willingness to engage in copper scrap recycling.</span></p></div><h2 id="qhbbxeajmn" class="contentTitle-b18c0982"><span></span>Geopolitical uncertainty will drive up copper inventories.</h2><div class="contentText-f9a0dc29"><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">In addition, Citi pointed out that as global trade frictions and geopolitical uncertainty have risen in recent years, governments' willingness to actively increase metal inventories has strengthened, a factor that will also impact copper prices. According to Citi's calculations, if global refined copper inventories rise from the current level of approximately 1.3 months of consumption to 2 months, with the inventory build completed within two years, the required copper price would be roughly $14,423/tonne; if it rises to 3 months of consumption, the price needed for a two-year build would reach as high as $27,885/tonne.</span></p><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="white-space: pre-wrap;">When governments increase copper inventories for strategic reserve purposes, not only does demand rise, but spot market liquidity also tightens, thereby amplifying price volatility and pushing up the spot premium relative to futures.</span></p></div><div class="contentText-f9a0dc29"><p class="PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph" dir="ltr"><span style="color: rgb(155, 155, 155); white-space: pre-wrap;">This content was translated using AI and reviewed for clarity. It is for informational purposes only.</span></p></div></div><a href="https://www.tradingkey.com/zh-hans/analysis/commodities/more/261880338-copper-cu-record-high-hormuz-15000-tradingkey" class="sourceLink-c53dbf65">View Original</a><div class="tips-e7dcdb05">Disclaimer: The content of this article solely represents the author's personal opinions and does not reflect the official stance of Tradingkey. Every report and analysis reflects an objective representation grounded in professional judgment.<br />Accuracy of information: We strive to present content accurately and clearly. Every piece of information undergoes rigorous verification, ensuring that investors’ decisions are built on a firm foundation of facts.","home_about_policy_content2":"All TradingKey content is prepared by editors working closely with financial experts. Drawing on broad knowledge and practical investment experience, they provide clear, professional, and impartial analyses of data and the underlying logic and trends behind market events, offering investors relevant decision-making reference.<br />Our editorial team works around the clock, monitoring authoritative sources, evaluating content, cross-referencing and double-checking data and facts—avoiding errors at the source and presenting users with objective, well-substantiated content.<br />Our financial expert team calibrates each piece for both professional rigour and neutrality. We reject vague, biased, or misleading expressions, ensuring every viewpoint offered is clear, rigorous, and impartial—truly honouring users’ trust.","home_about_policy_content3":"With academic backgrounds in finance, economics, or journalism, our editorial team is proficient and professional in macroeconomic policies, capital markets, and financial analysis. Your computer stores Cookies in a file in your web browser similar to a log book keeping records of your activities when you visit the site. When you visit the same website, it allows them to identify you easily and provide improved browsing experiences. Cookies do not transfer viruses or malware to your computer, and they do not affect the way your computer works. We may obtain your information through cookies when you visit our website. Different types of cookies record different activities.","home_cookie_content2":"We use cookies to analyse how users use our website to track and improve the website functionality efficiency and reliability. This allows us to quickly identify and resolve any issues that may arise to provide a high-quality customer experience. For Example: We may use performance cookies to track which pages of our website are most popular and which links between pages are most effective. With this information, we can enhance your navigation within our website, ease traffic and minimise performance disruptions you may experience. Cookies can also track whether you have been referred to our site from another website which can improve our advertising campaigns you accordingly. Another use of cookies includes storing your current login session. When you log in to the member's area, a 'session cookie' is set to remember the login session so that when you navigate around the website as an active user, you will not be required to log in again until you leave the website or session expires. Our functionality cookies allow us to recognise you as a user, recall your login information, and personalised preferences and ensure that your information is secure. Whenever you visit our trading platform, your user name and language chosen are conveniently stored from the use of cookies that defaults to your previous preferences.","home_cookie_content3":"This website uses Google Analytics - a web analytics service provided by Google, Inc. (“Google”). Google Analytics will use the analytics cookie on your computer to analyse how users use the website. The information generated by the cookie about your website use (including your IP address) may be transmitted and stored by Google on its servers. Google may use this information to evaluate your use of the website to comply with reports on website activity and to provide other services relating to website activity and internet usage. Google may also transfer this information to third parties if required by law. Google will not associate your IP address with any other data. Please use the data with caution for reference purposes only.","stock.score.scoring.explained.scoring":"Scoring Explained:<br/><br/>Scoring Principles:<br/>Scoring principles and dimensions are kept consistent for all stocks.\nAll component dimensions and the overall scores are consistently rated on a scale from 0 to 10, with 10 being the highest possible score. Separate dimension scores are weighted to calculate the overall score.<br/>If a stock does not meet the required data criteria or standards, no score will be assigned.<br/><br/>Scoring Dimensions:<br/>Financial Health (30% weight):<br/>A comprehensive score based on five equally weighted indicators: financial health, growth potential, profitability quality, operational efficiency, and shareholder returns. A higher score indicates better financial health.<br/><br/>Valuation (20% weight):<br/>The current value level based on two valuation indicators: industry valuation and stock valuation.<br/><br/>Forecast (10% weight):<br/>Market's confidence in the company's future profitability, scored based on the number and direction of analyst ratings.<br/><br/>Momentum (20% weight):<br/>The company's short-term price trend, analysed using technical indicators such as pressure and support levels, based on historical relative position.<br/><br/>Institutions (10% weight):<br/>The level of recognition from institutional investors, evaluated based on the proportion of institutional ownership and changes in institutional holdings.<br/><br/>Risk (10% weight):<br/>Risk status scored based on the US Dollar Risk factor (DXY) and benchmarked to a 240-day maximum drawdown of 50%. A higher score indicates lower risk; a lower score implies higher risk.","stock.score.related.info":"Industry at a Glance","stock.score.stock.score":"Score","stock.score.previous":"Previous score","stock.score.change":"Change","stock.score.3years":"3 years","stock.score.5years":"5 years","stock.score.240days":"240 days","stock.score.60days":"60 days","stock.score.120days":"120 days","stock.score.market.wide.ranking":"Overall Ranking","stock.score.industry.involved":"Industry","stock.score.current.score":"Current score","stock.score.rating":"Score","stock.score.Industry.average":"Industry Average","stock.score.financial.forecasting":"Financial Forecasting","stock.score.actual":"Actual","stock.score.estimate":"Forecast","stock.score.240maximum.drawdown":"240-Day Maximum Drawdown","stock.score.240maximum.daily.downside":"240-Day Volatility","stock.score.20days":"20 days","stock.score.na":"N/A","stock.score.indicator":"Key Metrics","tk_center_watch_key_insights":"Key Insights","tk_center_watch_remove":"Remove","tk_center_watchlist":"Watchlist","stock.score.overvalued.area":"Overvalued","stock.score.reasonable.area":"Fairly valued","stock.score.underestimation.area":"Undervalued","tk_center_watch_to_add":"Add a Symbol","company.right.icon":"→","stock.score.title.health":"Overall Financial Health","stock.score.title.valuation":"Company Valuation","tk_stock_analysis_risk_warning":"A higher score indicates lower risk; a lower score implies higher risk.","tk_stock_analysis_no_data":"No stock score available due to insufficient data.","nav.header.stock.score":"Stock <span>Score</span>","tk_not_found_title":"Oops! financing activities. It highlights how well the company manages cash to support growth and meet its obligations.","market_stock_analysis_q1":"How does TradingKey generate the Stock Score of {stockname}?","market_stock_analysis_q2":"How do we generate the financial health score of {stockname}?","market_stock_analysis_q3":"How do we generate the company valuation score of {stockname}?","market_stock_analysis_q4":"How do we generate the earnings forecast score of {stockname}?","market_stock_analysis_q5":"How do we generate the price momentum score of {stockname}?","market_stock_analysis_q6":"How do we generate the institutional confidence score of {stockname}?","market_stock_analysis_q7":"How do we generate the risk management score of {stockname}?","market_stock_analysis_a1":"The TradingKey Stock Score provides a comprehensive assessment of {stockname} based on 34 indicators derived from over 100 underlying data points. </br>These indicators are categorised into six key dimensions: financial health, company profile, earnings forecasts, price momentum, institutional confidence, and risk assessment, collectively providing investors with a thorough analysis of {stockname}’s performance and outlook.","market_stock_analysis_a2":"To generate the financial health score of {stockname}, we analyse various key financial indicators across several core dimensions, including financial fundamentals, earnings quality, operational efficiency, growth potential, and shareholder returns. </br>By integrating these comprehensive data,  the financial health score not only reflects {stockname}'s overall stability, operational efficiency, and growth potential but also captures the investment returns delivered to shareholders. This score aims to support rational decision-making for investors and other stakeholders. A higher score indicates a better financial health of {stockname}.","market_stock_analysis_a3":"To generate the company valuation score of {stockname}, we use several key financial ratios, including P/E, P/B, P/S, and P/CF. These metrics compare {stockname}’s market value to its earnings, assets, sales, and cash flow, serving as core indicators for assessing company value. </br>By analysing these ratios in an all-round manner, the valuation score reflects not only the market’s current perception of {stockname}’s value but also its future profitability and growth potential, thereby supporting more informed investment decisions. A higher score indicates a fairer valuation of {stockname}.","market_stock_analysis_a4":"To calculate the earnings forecast score of {stockname}, we consider various key indicators such as analyst ratings, financial forecasts, support and resistance levels, and peer comparisons:</br>Current ratings and price targets directly reflect analysts’ expectations, while the upside space indicates their views on the stock’s growth potential.</br>Financial metrics like earnings per share (EPS), revenue, net income, and earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT) are core measures for assessing profitability.</br>By analysing the high, median, and low ranges of the target price, we can identify price trends and determine key support and resistance levels.</br>Comparing these indicators against peers provides context for the stock’s performance and helps\nanchor more realistic expectations.</br>These factors together form a comprehensive earnings forecast score that accurately reflects the stock’s expected financial performance and growth potential. A higher score suggests that analysts have a more optimistic outlook on {stockname}’s future.","market_stock_analysis_a5":"When generating the price momentum score for {stockname}, we examine momentum indicators including support and resistance levels as well as volume metrics.</br>Support levels represent price points where buying interest is strong enough to prevent further declines, while resistance levels indicate prices where selling pressure may hinder upward movement. By analysing typical price volume alongside total volume, we assess overall market activity and liquidity. These indicators help identify price trends and potential shifts, enabling more accurate predictions of future earnings.</br>This comprehensive approach can reflect market sentiment and enable rational forecasts of {stockname}’s prices. A higher score indicates a more stable short-term price trend for {stockname}.","market_stock_analysis_a6":"To generate the institutional confidence score of {stockname}, we analyse both the proportion of shares held by institutions and the number of shares they own. A higher institutional ownership percentage indicates strong confidence of professional investors, which is generally a positive signal about {stockname}’s potential. By examining the number of shares held, we gain deeper insight into institutions’ commitment and attitude toward the stock, as institutional investors typically conduct thorough analysis before making investment decisions. Understanding institutional confidence helps us refine earnings forecasts and provides a more reliable perspective on the company’s future performance and market stability. A higher score indicates greater institutional endorsement of {stockname}.","market_stock_analysis_a7":"To assess the risk management score of {stockname}, we examine multiple key indicators related to returns, risk, volatility, and liquidity.</br>The highest and lowest daily returns reflect the potential size of gains and losses, while the Sharpe ratio measures risk-adjusted return performance. On the risk side, we analyse maximum drawdown and the return-to-drawdown ratio to identify extreme loss scenarios. Skewness helps assess the distribution of returns and whether performance may be biased. Volatility indicators—such as realised volatility and standardised true range—reveal price fluctuations. Downside risk-adjusted returns provide insight into potential losses and gains. Lastly, liquidity metrics like average turnover rate and turnover deviation indicate how actively the stock is traded.</br>These indicators together provide a multi-dimensional understanding of {stockname}’s risk-return profile, serving as core factors of the risk management score. A higher score indicates lower risk on the side of {stockname}.","markets_company_single_instituion":"{shareholder} holds {sharesheld} shares, accounting for {percentage}% of the total Citi: If Strait of Hormuz Is Unblocked, Price Will Rise to $15,000 by Year-End","description":"TradingKey - Despite the ongoing US-Iran war and the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz, copper prices maintain a solid floor. This Monday, LME (London Metal Exchange) three-month copper reached $13,637 per tonne at one point, marking its highest level since January 29. On the same day, LME three-month copper recorded a peak closing price of $13,952/tonne, with intraday prices briefly rising above $14,500/tonne.","topicName":"Commodities","subTopicName":"More","class":"FEATURED","author":{"id":"645","name":"Jane Zhang","route":"JaneZhang","avatar":"https://resource.tradingkey.com/uploads/20251024/jane-zhang-96fc9d97aa614d6f8b9d61d58b50d787.jpg"},"organization":{"id":"41","name":"TradingKey","route":"tradingkey","avatar":"https://resource.tradingkey.com/uploads/20251024/68658377675a42e2bbed98144a729ddatradingkeylogo-ed79a44cf96240a287ca1bb541f06bff.png"},"publishAt":"1778504108","source":"TradingKey","updateAt":"1778504232","contents":[{"title":"","content":"<div id=\"thirdPartyWrapContainer\"><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">TradingKey - Despite the prolonged U.S.-Iran war and the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz, copper price bottoms remain firm. This Monday, LME (London Metal Exchange) three-month copper touched $13,637 per tonne, reaching its highest level since January 29. On that day, LME three-month copper recorded a closing high of $13,952 per tonne, having briefly surged above $14,500 per tonne during the session.</span></p><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">Citi's latest report noted that the copper market continues to demonstrate resilience under the pressure of war and ongoing blockades. Citi maintained its near-term price target at $13,000 per tonne and predicted that once the strait reopens and market sentiment improves, copper prices will rise to $15,000 per tonne by year-end.</span></p><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">Citi believes that the reason copper has not fallen significantly despite the war is primarily due to support from three factors: the energy transition, growth in AI and military demand, and supply-side constraints.</span></p></div>"},{"title":"Copper Price Forecast: $12,000 Floor in Q2, Potential Surge to $15,000 by Year-End","content":"<p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">In the report, Citi forecasts that under a base-case scenario, the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will persist until the end of May. Amid suppressed global growth demand, even a more significant sell-off in risk assets would likely see dip-buying in the copper market support prices above $12,000 per ton through the second quarter.</span></p><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">In a bull-case scenario, Citi predicts that if the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz drives improved growth expectations and the release of restocking demand, or if the crisis provides a structural boost to energy transition demand, average copper prices could reach $15,000 per ton by year-end. In a bear-case scenario, Citi expects copper prices to fall to $10,000 per ton, but assigns this outcome a weighting of only 20%.</span></p>"},{"title":"Why High Oil Prices are Failing to Hinder the Rise in Copper Prices?","content":"<p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">Generally, there is a negative correlation between oil prices and copper prices, as rising oil prices significantly increase manufacturing costs and stifle global economic growth. Copper is regarded as a bellwether for global industrial activity; when economic growth is hit, demand for industrial copper also declines, exerting downward pressure on copper prices. However, in this round of conflict, copper prices have continued to rise overall, gaining approximately 10% since late 2025.</span></p><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">Analysis indicates that the primary reason is that the expansion of structural demand for copper has substantially mitigated the downward risk to total copper demand. Historical data shows that during major economic downturns, such as the second oil shock in the 1980s and the 2008 global financial crisis, copper demand fell by an average of 3% to 5% annually. According to Citi's estimates, assuming structural demand remains constant, if cyclical demand drops by 5%, global refined copper consumption would only decrease by about 1.7%; if cyclical demand falls by just 3%, total global demand would remain largely flat.</span></p><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">Citi even pointed out that, in some cases, high oil prices do not lead to a contraction in copper demand; instead, they may drive copper demand higher by accelerating the energy transition. For instance, under such geopolitical pressure, fuel-importing nations may promote the adoption of renewable energy, naturally increasing copper demand for electric vehicles, energy storage, and power grid infrastructure.</span></p>"},{"title":"Military demand provides additional upside potential for copper prices.","content":"<p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">Citi noted another factor in its report: the support for copper prices from military demand. According to Citi's estimates, global military-related copper consumption is approximately 2.5 million tons per year, accounting for about 9% of total global consumption. Furthermore, a global consensus has emerged that international defense budgets will maintain moderate growth, which will support military copper demand remaining at elevated levels.</span></p><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">The equipment intensity of modern warfare continues to increase, with the extensive use of consumable hardware such as drones and missiles accelerating copper consumption. Historical data suggests that following the outbreak of the Russia-Ukraine war, the growth rate of military copper consumption may outpace the growth rate of actual military expenditures.</span></p>"},{"title":"Surging Energy Costs Pressure Scrap Copper Recycling","content":"<p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">While copper demand undergoes structural reshaping, its supply side is also being impacted by strait closures. Currently, copper is sourced from two channels: copper scrap recycling and primary copper ore production. Since the beginning of the year, copper mine production expectations have remained largely flat, making scrap recovery and recycling the primary factor influencing copper output.</span></p><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">However, the collection, processing, and remelting of copper scrap are highly dependent on energy and transportation, making a closure of the Strait of Hormuz a critical factor affecting copper production. Such a closure would not only drive up energy prices but also push up transport insurance premiums, fuel surcharges, and other transit fees, thereby dampening suppliers' willingness to engage in copper scrap recycling.</span></p>"},{"title":"Geopolitical uncertainty will drive up copper inventories.","content":"<p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">In addition, Citi pointed out that as global trade frictions and geopolitical uncertainty have risen in recent years, governments' willingness to actively increase metal inventories has strengthened, a factor that will also impact copper prices. According to Citi's calculations, if global refined copper inventories rise from the current level of approximately 1.3 months of consumption to 2 months, with the inventory build completed within two years, the required copper price would be roughly $14,423/tonne; if it rises to 3 months of consumption, the price needed for a two-year build would reach as high as $27,885/tonne.</span></p><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">When governments increase copper inventories for strategic reserve purposes, not only does demand rise, but spot market liquidity also tightens, thereby amplifying price volatility and pushing up the spot premium relative to futures.</span></p>"},{"title":"","content":"<p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"color: rgb(155, 155, 155); white-space: pre-wrap;\">This content was translated using AI and reviewed for clarity. It is for informational purposes only.</span></p>"}],"editor":null,"faq":[],"isLocked":false,"originalUrl":"","isOriginal":true,"translationSourceUrl":"/zh-hans/analysis/commodities/more/261880338-copper-cu-record-high-hormuz-15000-tradingkey","videoInfo":null,"relatedArticles":[{"langCode":"zh-hans","fullPath":"/analysis/commodities/more/261880338-copper-cu-record-high-hormuz-15000-tradingkey","isOriginal":true}],"isStockCategory":false,"stats":{"likes":0,"favorites":0,"comments":0,"shares":0,"views":4541,"isLiked":false}},"contentList":[{"title":"","content":"<div id=\"thirdPartyWrapContainer\"><p class=\"PlaygroundEditorTheme__paragraph\" dir=\"ltr\"><span style=\"white-space: pre-wrap;\">TradingKey - Despite the prolonged U.S.-Iran war and the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz, copper price bottoms remain firm.
